ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic toast entry (FOODON_00002473). No specific entry exists for kaya toast or flavoured kaya toast variants. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and similar products. No specific code exists for kaya toast or raspberry vanilla toast. Closest parent category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for kaya toast or flavoured toast variants. SNOMED CT does not have a suitable code for this food.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Raspberry Vanilla Kaya Toast consists of two slices of toasted white bread spread with raspberry-vanilla kaya and butter, weighing approximately 90 g.

nutrient_source.title: Kaya Toast, white bread with kaya spread — adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for white toast and coconut jam
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Raspberry Vanilla Kaya Toast exists in HPB Foodo. Values are adapted from HPB Foodo kaya toast reference (plain kaya toast, white bread) combined with USDA SR Legacy data for white bread (NDB 18069) and coconut jam/kaya. Raspberry and vanilla flavourings contribute negligible macronutrient change. Per-100g values represent the composite toasted bread with kaya and butter spread, consistent with Toast Box-style preparation. Energy calculated from macros using Atwater factors.

gi_source.title: White bread GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Raspberry Vanilla Kaya Toast. GI of 70 is based on white bread GI (mean 70, range 64–74) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Kaya spread adds sugar which does not substantially lower the GI of the composite. Raspberry and vanilla flavourings are negligible in quantity. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 44.6) / 100 ≈ 31.2, where carbohydrate per 90 g serving = 49.5 × 0.9 = 44.6 g.

hypertension.guidance: Each 90 g serving provides approximately 252 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to one serving and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods such as soft-boiled eggs with soy sauce to keep daily sodium within the recommended 2000 mg limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index and glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 70 and a glycemic load of approximately 31 per serving, Raspberry Vanilla Kaya Toast causes a rapid postprandial blood glucose rise and should be consumed only occasionally by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, ideally paired with a protein source to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and refined carbohydrate content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of approximately 4.7 g saturated fat and 44.6 g refined carbohydrate per serving contributes to atherogenic risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and consider wholegrain alternatives to reduce saturated fat and glycemic burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Raspberry Vanilla Kaya Toast is a flavoured variant of the classic Singaporean kaya toast, served at Toast Box, a popular Singaporean café chain. It consists of two slices of white bread that are toasted until golden and crisp, then spread generously with a raspberry-vanilla infused kaya — a traditional Southeast Asian coconut egg jam — along with a layer of cold butter. The raspberry and vanilla flavourings give the kaya a fruity, floral sweetness that distinguishes it from the classic pandan-coconut kaya. The texture contrasts crispy toast with a soft, sweet, and slightly sticky spread. Kaya toast is a cornerstone of the Singaporean breakfast culture, typically served alongside soft-boiled eggs seasoned with soy sauce and white pepper, and a cup of kopi (local coffee) or teh (tea). Nutritionally, this item is energy-dense at approximately 310 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content (49.5 g/100 g) dominated by refined starch and added sugars from the kaya spread. The glycemic index is high (GI ≈ 70), making it a significant glycemic load food per serving. Saturated fat from butter and coconut-based kaya contributes approximately 5.2 g per 100 g. Clinically, this food is of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its high GI and GL, and for those with cardiovascular disease due to its saturated fat and refined carbohydrate content. Sodium at 280 mg per 100 g warrants attention for hypertensive individuals. It is suitable for vegetarians and is generally considered halal.
common_user_queries: What is raspberry vanilla kaya toast?
How many calories in Toast Box raspberry vanilla toast?
Is kaya toast high in sugar?
Is Toast Box kaya toast halal?
What is the GI of kaya toast?
meal_context: paired with soft-boiled eggs and kopi

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Raspberry Vanilla Kaya Toast exists in HPB Foodo or any approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo plain kaya toast and USDA SR Legacy white bread and coconut jam data. The raspberry and vanilla flavourings are present in small quantities and do not materially alter the macronutrient profile. GI is estimated from the white bread GI reference in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy source data for this food category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the source data for kaya toast or white bread with spread in the approved sources. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B vitamins, folate, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, and zinc are estimated from USDA SR Legacy white bread and coconut jam composite data and are approximate. The raspberry and vanilla flavourings used by Toast Box are proprietary; their exact contribution to sugar content is not publicly disclosed but is assumed to be minor relative to the kaya base. Serving size of 90 g is an estimate for two standard slices of kaya toast as served at Toast Box. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white bread from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). GL per serving = (70 × 44.6) / 100 ≈ 31.2.
